# buildkit_image (Resource)

Builds a container image from a Dockerfile with BuildKit and optionally pushes it to one or more registries in a single build. Supports multi-platform builds, build args, labels, build secrets, SSH agent forwarding, cache import/export, and SBOM/provenance attestations.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Build a multi-platform image and push it to a registry, with an SBOM and
# max-mode provenance attestation, build args, a build secret, and registry
# cache. Credentials come from the provider's registry_auth or docker config.

resource "buildkit_image" "app" {
  context    = "${path.module}/app"
  dockerfile = "Dockerfile"
  platforms  = ["linux/amd64", "linux/arm64"]
  target     = "runtime"

  args = {
    NODE_ENV = "production"
  }

  labels = {
    "org.opencontainers.image.source" = "https://github.com/org/app"
  }

  secrets = {
    npm_token = var.npm_token
  }

  publish {
    registry   = "ghcr.io"
    repository = "org/app"
    tags       = ["latest", var.version]
  }

  cache_from {
    type  = "registry"
    attrs = { ref = "ghcr.io/org/app:buildcache" }
  }

  cache_to {
    type  = "registry"
    attrs = { ref = "ghcr.io/org/app:buildcache", mode = "max" }
  }

  attestations {
    sbom       = true
    provenance = "max"
  }

  # Rebuild when the context content changes.
  triggers = {
    context = data.buildkit_context.app.digest
  }
}

data "buildkit_context" "app" {
  path = "${path.module}/app"
}

output "image_digest" {
  value = buildkit_image.app.image_digest
}

output "pushed" {
  value = buildkit_image.app.published
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `context` (String) Path to the build context directory.
- `dockerfile` (String) Path to the Dockerfile, relative to `context`.
- `platforms` (Set of String) Target platforms, e.g. `["linux/amd64", "linux/arm64"]`.

### Optional

- `args` (Map of String) Build args (values for `ARG` instructions).
- `attestations` (Block, Optional) Attach SBOM and/or provenance attestations to the build output. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--attestations))
- `cache_from` (Block List) Cache import sources (`--import-cache`). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--cache_from))
- `cache_to` (Block List) Cache export targets (`--export-cache`). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--cache_to))
- `forward_ssh_agent_socket` (Boolean) Forward the host `SSH_AUTH_SOCK` as the `default` ssh mount (`RUN --mount=type=ssh`).
- `labels` (Map of String) Image labels (equivalent to `LABEL` instructions).
- `publish` (Block List) A registry/repository and the tags to publish. Multiple blocks publish to multiple targets in a single build. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--publish))
- `secrets` (Map of String, Sensitive) Build secrets in `id => value` form, exposed via `RUN --mount=type=secret,id=...`.
- `secrets_base64` (Map of String, Sensitive) Build secrets in `id => base64(value)` form, decoded before use.
- `ssh` (Block List) Explicit ssh agent/socket forwards (`RUN --mount=type=ssh,id=...`). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--ssh))
- `target` (String) Multi-stage build target to build.
- `triggers` (Map of String) Arbitrary map; any change forces the image to be rebuilt.

### Read-Only

- `context_digest` (String) Dockerignore-aware sha256 of the build context at build time.
- `id` (String) Synthetic resource id (equals `image_digest`).
- `image_digest` (String) The sha256 digest of the built image manifest.
- `published` (List of Object) Resolved publish coordinates, including digest URLs. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--published))

<a id="nestedblock--attestations"></a>
### Nested Schema for `attestations`

Optional:

- `provenance` (String) Provenance mode: `min` or `max`.
- `sbom` (Boolean) Generate an SBOM attestation.


<a id="nestedblock--cache_from"></a>
### Nested Schema for `cache_from`

Required:

- `type` (String) Cache type: `registry`, `local`, `gha`, or `inline`.

Optional:

- `attrs` (Map of String) Type-specific attributes (e.g. `ref`, `mode`, `dest`, `src`).


<a id="nestedblock--cache_to"></a>
### Nested Schema for `cache_to`

Required:

- `type` (String) Cache type: `registry`, `local`, `gha`, or `inline`.

Optional:

- `attrs` (Map of String) Type-specific attributes (e.g. `ref`, `mode`, `dest`, `src`).


<a id="nestedblock--publish"></a>
### Nested Schema for `publish`

Required:

- `registry` (String) Registry host, e.g. `docker.io` or `ghcr.io`.
- `repository` (String) Repository name, e.g. `org/app`.
- `tags` (List of String) Tags to publish.

Optional:

- `insecure` (Boolean) Allow pushing over plain HTTP / untrusted TLS (sets `registry.insecure=true`). Also used for digest lookups during refresh.
- `push` (Boolean) Push to the registry. Defaults to `true`.


<a id="nestedblock--ssh"></a>
### Nested Schema for `ssh`

Required:

- `id` (String) SSH mount id.
- `paths` (List of String) Socket or key paths.


<a id="nestedatt--published"></a>
### Nested Schema for `published`

Read-Only:

- `digest_url` (String)
- `insecure` (Boolean)
- `registry` (String)
- `repository` (String)
- `tag` (String)
- `tag_url` (String)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# buildkit_image is imported by its digest URL (registry/repo@sha256:...).
terraform import buildkit_image.app ghcr.io/org/app@sha256:abc123...
```
